body_txt: A lot of characters, no matter what they've done, where they've gone, or who they are, need therapy. But sometimes they don't have the means to find an actual counselor, psychiatrist, or therapist. Or, even worse, they may live in a world/society where There Are No Therapists . So, to help their friends, another character (usually The Confidant or The Heart ) may take up the role of a therapist or counselor (sometimes accidentally, sometimes intentionally). They'll encourage the troubled character to talk about what's bothering them, help them work through their feelings, and possibly suggest coping mechanisms or other ways they can get better. If they're really trying to embrace the role of amateur therapist, then they may even dress the part and refer to themselves as a doctor. The Mentor , Old Master , Cool Old Guy , Good Shepherd , Psychologist Teacher , A Father to His Men , or a character Wise Beyond Their Years is also likely to be this trope, as their experience often gives them the wisdom/patience needed to help someone talk out their problems. They will often be the one to first realize that another character needs help and will likely help start them on their Mental Health Recovery Arc . Not to be mistaken for an amateur physical therapist; this character helps others with emotional problems. This trope will likely occur in a Therapy Episode . It is a subtrope of Uncertified Expert and a Super-Trope of Bartender Confidant , who tends to the mental and social health needs of his clientele from behind the bar. Compare with Magical Minority Person and Working Out Their Emotions . Contrast with There Are No Therapists , Psycho Psychologist , The Shrink (specifically those that give bad advice to extort money), and Therapy Is for the Weak . May cross over with Therapist in Therapy , especially if the therapist is getting counseling from a friend who isn't a licensed therapist. See also Warrior Psychologist .
